title: A Mahommadan Ship Fireman.
content:
Up from the oven pit,
        The hell where poor men toil,
     At the sunset hour he comes
        Clean-clothed, washed from soil.

     On the fo'c's'le head he kneels,
        His face to the hallowed West.
     He prays, and bows and prays.
        Does he pray for death and rest?
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Francis William Lauderdale Adams delivers a powerful performance in "A Mahommadan Ship Fireman."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
